question: Reconstruct Finnveden, Riedel, and Shulman's argument for how AGI enables lock-in via multiple distinct mechanisms. Name the mechanisms and explain how they combine to make lock-in possible.
required_elements: Lock-in operates through multiple distinct mechanisms, not a single pathway
Preservation of goals through stored minds or specifications that can survive indefinitely
Preventing value drift through constraints on information exposure (potentially including halting philosophical progress)
Preventing disruption through surveillance and control to block unauthorized space travel, weapons, or competitors
These mechanisms combine so that an institution could practically eliminate internal sources of drift
Therefore AGI enables lock-in through coordinated technological, informational, and enforcement mechanisms
source_passage: Given all these options, it seems more likely than not that an institution could practically eliminate any internal sources of drift that it wanted to.

metadata.note: The three mechanisms (preservation, preventing value drift, preventing disruption) are discussed across separate sections of the paper rather than enumerated in a single passage. The required_elements reflect the substantive argument structure.
